REW_PAY_005.semantics: Ideal pay market position — peaked on On-Market (median-targeters dominate). Seeds over ANSWERING orgs only (N/A-Don't-know option stripped per standing rule; 28 answers n-excluded). STRATEGY-CONFIG metric (Diff 4): never pools, no verdict — reshape only, ruled 2026-07-18 ①.

EXT_REW_GAP_010.semantics: Remote-work location permission — Anywhere-UK dominant, international a thin tail. Don't-know stripped (4 answers n-excluded). ANCHORED BUT NON-DIRECTIONAL (ruled 2026-07-18 ②): no honest better/worse pole; renders prevalence, no verdict.

REW265_PAY_RANGEMAX.semantics: Range-max policy RECOMPUTED (r3s3 coupling): conditioned on the PER-ORG have-ranges set from REW_PAY_001's maturity assignment (strict Yes). Members split lump/continue/frozen 40/20/40; non-members answer 'No formal ranges'. PER-ORG COHERENCE IS THE HARD ASSERT (zero have-ranges!=Yes with a policy answer) — the pay-structure-family guard. Durable by construction: RANGEMAX is orderless-nominal (engine skip, never re-paired) and REW_PAY_001 re-derives hash-deterministically. Aggregate here is the freeze-gate tier-2b cover (~45/22/22/11).

REW_PAY_TIPS_EXIST_7c80c508.semantics: Tips/service charges WITHIN THE SECTOR SCOPE only (r3s4): sector_scopes.json hides the metric entirely outside Hospitality; the 199 out-of-scope answers (the impossible 67.5%% all-org yes) are DELETED, not NA'd. Within the 15 hospitality orgs: 80/20 operate/don't. Freeze tier-2b checks over in-scope answers (all answers are in-scope by construction post-clean).

REW264_HLT_VIRTUALGP.source: CIPD Health & Wellbeing 2025 (Mar-Apr 2025 fieldwork, YouGov n=1,101 employers), large-private 250+ band
REW264_HLT_VIRTUALGP.semantics: VIRTUAL GP RE-ANCHOR (r3sw7, ruled): total provision SOURCED grade A/B — CIPD H&W 2025 large-private band: 54% all-staff / 69% any-form -> No ~31%. BASIS CORRECTION: the old 77% No was anchored all-sizes-incl-micro; the cohort is 250-999 = the large-private band. Standalone-vs-Via-PMI-only split EST (paywalled decomposition), CONSTRAINED by the sourced fact virtual GP (54%) > PMI (40%) for large-private -> Via-PMI-only is a minority (11). SIZE GRADIENT RECORDED NOT GATED (SME 23% / large 54% on register as reference; cohort uniformly one band, seeded flat cohort-centre). VERDICT RE-EARNS: first benefits metric back through the Diff-14 path — unbenchmarked lifts, provision verdict renders.

REW_BEN_HOL_001.semantics: ANNUAL-LEAVE SECTOR FLOOR (ruled): Retail/Hospitality/Logistics modal = Statutory 20 days (20+8BH = 28 = UK minimum), floor-band dist 45/32/18/4/1 (Claude derivation of the modal-20 ruling, presented at gate; floor-band DKs get real values — band-internal DK strip, flagged). SURGICAL-BAND: standard sectors byte-untouched. SUPERSEDES the global sme_large 0.553 CIPD marginal — the cohort over-weights floor sectors (84/220 = 38%) vs CIPD's economy-wide mix, so a sector-honest seed reads global 25+ ~47% (composition argument, presented for ruling). NEW-EMPLOYEE basis. TENURE explicitly NOT handled (public/charity stay 25-standard, known-approximate pending the tenure-banding mechanism — queued like location-capture).

PROP_fe1a29ec.positive_option: Yes - formal/structured (survey data, provider, market analysis)
remainder_options: Informally only (job adverts, ad hoc, online, ChatGPT)
PROP_fe1a29ec.semantics: External benchmarking on the MATURITY GRADIENT (r3s2 pilot, ruled): formal% keyed per-org on HR_Maturity - Basic 15 / Developing 60 / Advanced 90 (EST, D. Whitfield). Replaces the Diff-15 flat 60/30/10 cohort-centre placeholder. Remainder informal:none shifts by band (2:1/3:1/4:1 - informal higher at lower maturity, derivation presented for ruling). Maturity-not-size keying per David's principle; a small-but-Advanced org correctly reads high.

REW_FAI_128.semantics: Top-performer market pricing on the maturity gradient (r3s3): Basic 5 / Developing 25 / Advanced 70 -> cohort ~44%% Yes (was 59%% engine-flat). No sector gate (any sector can be maturity-mature). Remainder No:Planned 8:1/8:1/3:1 (Planned share rises with maturity among non-doers — derivation presented for ruling). Stays verdict-suppressed EST.

REW_PAY_001.semantics: Formal pay ranges (have-ranges) on the maturity gradient (r3s3): Basic 5 / Developing 30 / Advanced 90 -> cohort ~55%% Yes (supersedes the Diff-15 flat 40%% conditioning). STRICT-Yes defines the have-ranges set for the RANGEMAX coupling (Partially does NOT count — a partially-ranged org answers 'No formal ranges' on range-max; presented for ruling). Remainder Partially:No 1:3/1:1/2:1 (partial adoption rises with maturity among non-full adopters). Stays verdict-suppressed EST.

REW_INC_103.semantics: Bonus-eligible %% on the SECTOR-KEYED gradient (r3sw2 ruled): bonus EXISTS Commercial 90 / Charity 20 / Public 5; breadth among havers Commercial broad (75%%+ modal) / Charity moderate (~50) / Public narrow (frontline-perf). Corrects the Diff-13 absolute charity/public exclusion — low-but-nonzero is the reality. Band splits are my derivation of David's two-stage figures, presented at the gate. within_band=latent (coherence spine; hash stays maturity-only per the Step-2 ruling scope). Self-heals re-tag drift by re-derivation.

REW26_BEN_PENSION_TYPE.source: TPR Occupational DB landscape 2025 (data 31 Mar 2025) — 200 public-service schemes, 19.78m members, open to new members by statute; CARE = standard DB (no separate TPR class)
REW26_BEN_PENSION_TYPE.semantics: PUBLIC-SECTOR CARVE-OUT (r3sw3 ruled, TPR grade A): public-band new joiners get CARE=DB 95 / DC 5; NO _default — commercial/charity bands are byte-untouched (surgical-band contract), staying at the frozen national 95/3/2. Tier-1 RE-FREEZE at the post-carve national aggregate (G7 baseline re-established, EAP precedent). CONSTRUCT FLAG: TPR counts schemes/members, Lumi asks org-offer — immaterial for the public band (statutory participation ~= offer). New-employee basis per the standing rule.

REW265_BEN_PMICOMP.terminal: None of these — core cover only
REW265_BEN_PMICOMP.base: PMI-holding orgs = REW_BEN_038 'Private Medical Insurance (PMI)' tickers (n=130 seed)
REW265_BEN_PMICOMP.source: Insurer product structure: Bupa/AXA Health/Aviva/Vitality corporate PMI tiers + broker guides (Drewberry, myTribe, Usay) — option list and standard-vs-optional classification sourced; per-option incidence EST, structure-anchored (standard-adjacent high, add-on low)
REW265_BEN_PMICOMP.semantics: PMI COVERAGE-COMPOSITION REDESIGN (r3sw8): replaces REW263_BEN_PMIMH (single-select, N/A 56.8% distorting the distribution). Multi-select over the APPLICABLE BASE ONLY — conditioned on PMI-exists, no N/A option, n = PMI-havers by construction (the N/A-not-on-graph pilot). Prevalences are INDEPENDENT per-option shares of PMI-havers (won't sum to 100) — the tier-2d incidence gate holds each at max(5pp, 1/n). 4 UNIVERSALS ASSUMED STANDARD, not benchmarked (in-patient, digital GP, cancer cover, basic MH support line — ~90%+ everywhere, stated in help_text). Terminal 'None of these — core cover only' is derived-exclusive (zero-pick orgs stay in n). VERDICT-SUPPRESSED (EST incidence); per-option re-earn path when by-option incidence is sourced.

coherence_pairs.note: operates-LTIP=Yes must be EXACTLY the orgs with any LTI-by-level eligibility (r3sw2 ruled, range-max pattern)
clawback-Yes must be a SUBSET of the bonus-exists set (r3sw5 ruled) — you cannot claw back a bonus you don't pay; prevents a future INC_103 reseed silently re-breaking the conditioning

PMI composition is CONDITIONED on PMI-exists (r3sw8 N/A-exclusion pilot): every org answering the composition multi-select must tick PMI in the benefits inventory — the applicable base IS the conditioning set; prevents a future REW_BEN_038 reseed silently widening the graph past the PMI-haver cohort
share-plan participation is answered only by plan operators (r3sw11 ruled): substantive answers ⊆ the any-plan set
SAYE discount exists only where SAYE does (r3sw11 ruled — the 12 SIP-only 20%-max artifacts repaired): substantive ⊆ the SAYE side
SIP elements exist only where a SIP does (r3sw11 ruled — 16 SAYE-only fabrications + 6 operator no-SIP contradictions repaired): substantive ⊆ the SIP side
share-capital has ONE authority — the parent's no-shares option (r3sw11 ruled, one-authority principle): EMICSOP substantive ⊆ parent share-capital set; the child's own N/A must never diverge again
EWA fee model exists only where EWA runs (r3sw12 ruled: OFFER-OR-PILOT grain — pilots are live schemes with real fee models): substantive ⊆ the 77-org offer-or-pilot set
EWA withdrawal caps exist only where EWA runs (r3sw12 ruled: OFFER-OR-PILOT grain): substantive ⊆ the 77-org offer-or-pilot set
PMI by-level eligibility is CONDITIONED on PMI-exists (r3sw14): only PMI-havers hold matrix rows — same parent as premium/composition; prevents a reseed re-widening the base past the haver cohort

_discipline: structured fields only; verbatim-validated; marginal only where earned; SALSAC/MENOPLAN/PLSA_QM guards asserted

RED_COST_01.why: context: value figure (statutory £751/wk cap, £22,530 max, age-banded formula) — no marginal; observed average cost per employee remains unanchored

RED_TERM_02.why: context: value figure (statutory cap £751/wk from Apr 2026, 30 weeks max) — no marginal; enhanced-offer distribution unanchored; seeded bands already Check-C fl

REW26_BEN_PLSA_QM.why: context: upper-bound proxy (<=0.29) — bound cannot set a target_share unless David rules to seed at the bound

REW26_BEN_SALSAC.why: do-not-emit: 0.54 is large-only AND measures offer-at-all, not default enrolment; if David rules the question means 'offered', blend 0.15/0.54 (pair on REW_BEN_

REW_BEN_038.why: CONTEXT not a single prevalence: this anchor is a compendium of ~15 separate benefit prevalences (each with its own all/large/SME split), i.e. a distribution across options of a multi-select question. Usable only if split into one structured row per benefit option; several entries are themselves ranges (e.g. PMI SME 17-18%) or large-only (e.g. free eye tests 52% large). Do not collapse to one number. Note internal near-duplicates with other rows (e.g. OSP 60% large here vs 60-66% in REW_BEN_SICK_001; EAP 10% all / 61-62% large here vs 67/78/39 in REW26_WEL_EAP - conflicting EAP figures across register rows, reviewer should reconcile sources).

REW_BEN_048.why: CONTEXT not a prevalence: 50-59% is a benefit LEVEL band (percent of salary), and 25.2% is the share of the conditional base (employers already offering IP) sitting in that modal band - conditional distribution, not an all-employer target share. The 34% 'offer no IP' figure is corroboration context only and conflicts in direction of expression with REW_BEN_046 (25% offer vs 34% offer none - different sources/bases, broker-book skew per base_field). Grade B, not nationally weighted.

REW_BEN_112.why: CONTEXT only: 68% is the share of employers inside a 3-6% contribution RANGE — a distribution statistic for a categorical level question, likely spanning multiple answer options, so unusable as a single target share. Broker-book skew toward larger/advised employers per base_field. Grade B.

REW_BEN_PENS_EE_MAX_01.why: CONTEXT only: all figures are contribution-rate LEVELS (median 6%, p25 5%, p75 7%, range 3-12%), not employer-prevalence percentages — the % signs denote salary percentages, not shares of employers. Tiny base (n=59) with large-employer/FTSE skew and pre-2024 vintage per base_field. Grade B.

REW_BEN_PENS_EMP_MAX_01.why: CONTEXT only: two OVERLAPPING cumulative thresholds (~50% at 9%+, 27% at 11%+; the 11%+ group is a subset of the 9%+ group) — these are CDF points for a banded level question, not option shares, and cannot seed a single marginal without the reviewer choosing a threshold. '~' approximate on the 50. Broker-book skew toward larger/advised employers per base_field. Grade B.

REW263_TIME_NEONATAL.why: context: statutory entitlement value (up to 12 wks from Apr 2025) - enhancement prevalence unmeasured; no marginal

REW263_TIME_PATPAY.why: context: self-selected benchmark-tier base - 0.67 is Bright Horizons top-tier (family-friendly benchmark participants), 0.21 broad measures a different quantity

REW263_TIME_PREGLOSS.why: context: legal-horizon note (statutory pregnancy-loss leave from 2027 under ERA 2025, currently discretionary; incoming right is UNPAID while the question asks

REW_BEN_FAM_006.why: context: statutory-floor values (SMP/SPP/ShPP GBP194.32/wk, SMP 6wks at 90% AWE, paternity 2wks, SPL 50wks, day-one rights) - legal baseline, no enhancement pre

REW_BEN_HOL_002.why: context: statutory-floor value (5.6 weeks / 28 days; employers MAY count the 8 bank holidays within it) - legal baseline, no prevalence measured; no marginal

REW_BEN_HOL_004.why: context: collective-agreement base (IDR Payline, 56/98 = 0.57; unionised, 84% 250+ FTE, ~51% public/voluntary) - unblendable subgroup on an all-cohort question;

REW_BEN_SICK_002.why: NOT a prevalence: duration distribution (~10% under 1 month, ~40% over 6 months) with conditional denominator 'orgs with OSP', approximate '~' figures, bands do not sum to 100. Question itself asks a duration ('for how long'), not a yes/no. 'Public sector most generous' is qualitative. Route to CONTEXT.

PROP_634adacd.why: NOT a prevalence: 3% is the median EXPECTED basic pay increase (forward-looking level/value). Seeding it as an employer share would be a category error. Route to CONTEXT for the salary-increase question's answer distribution.

PROP_9e4ad87f.why: context: value figure (pay-AWARD median 3%; band split 3-3.99% = 40% of employers, 5%+ = 15%) — award distribution, not budget-as-%-of-payroll; no marginal

PROP_dff9a2a5.why: context: value figure (~3% is median award SIZE from CIPD LMO Spring 2026, not a coverage share; '<0%/freeze small tail' is directional only) — no marginal

REW_BEN_058.why: context: forward-looking benefits-SPEND direction split (plan increase 8% / same 73% / decrease 6%), not a retrospective enhanced-in-last-12m prevalence — no ma

REW263_TIME_FERTLEAVE.note: | positive_from ruled 2026-07-16: 22% counts PAID only; Unpaid/flexible sits lean-side

option_order: Yes - unpaid sabbaticals or career breaks only
Support provided, but no specific paid leave

REW_BEN_REM_PAY_001.note: RULED: 'Treatment varies by role or case' is ordinal second-leanest (guarantees nothing -> less protective than a universal glide path; conservative/under-claims), ruled over the non-ordinal-permanent-context alternative. Supersedes the partial 2/6 ordering migrated from anchored_spikes.json. 'Not applicable'/'Don't know' are structural skips (CICOVER rule). Live DB flags 'varies' is_na=true (scoring exclusion) — engine reshapes by ordering membership only, so coexists; positive_from='Base pay is protected' per the ruled extraction semantics: 64% IS the protected share ('have not and do not intend to').

REW262_GOV_EQUALPAYAUDIT.note: | positive_from ruled 2026-07-16: Annually only — 'Ad hoc' excluded (ad hoc != a 12-month cycle; the 35% measures last-12-months)

No – study undertaken in personal time
Combination of enhanced leave and pay
Combination of enhanced sick pay and SSP

REW_BEN_SICK_004.na_note: 'Not applicable (statutory only)' is an NA-skip via CONDITIONING (child of SICK_001: no OSP -> out of scope), not by label

Yes - formal/structured (survey data, provider, market analysis)

REW_BEN_FAM_008.note: LIVE options are a 4-rung set (discovered at promotion; source implied a triple). positive_from at Partially-paid = ANY-PAID read matching the 86% source construct ('offer paid bereavement leave'); FLAG-FOR-CONFIRM in the delta review — strict alternative is positive_from at the top rung
